title: Discovery Service: Append-Only Audit Logging with Hash Chain Integrity and Export API
description: Implement a comprehensive, append-only, tamper-evident audit logging subsystem for the Discovery Service. Every admin mutation, credential lifecycle event, enrollment action, chain publish, and sentinel lifecycle event must produce an audit_event record. The records form a hash chain (each event includes the hash of the previous event) providing cryptographic tamper evidence. Export APIs allow security investigations with filtering, pagination, and optional signed export bundles.
objectives: Every security-relevant action is immutably recorded with actor identity, timestamp, and summary
Hash chain integrity allows detection of record tampering or deletion
Export API enables bulk export for SIEM integration and forensic investigation

steps: Step 1 - audit_events Table (Append-Only): Table defined in TASK-022. Schema here: id (UUID PK), event_id (TEXT UNIQUE — urn:uuid:... format), ts (TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL default now()), actor_type (TEXT NOT NULL — 'ADMIN'|'SENTINEL'|'SYSTEM'|'BREAK_GLASS'), actor_id (TEXT — user sub or sentinel DID), action (TEXT NOT NULL — enum of audit actions), target_type (TEXT — 'service'|'sentinel'|'credential'|'enrollment_token'|'status_list'|...), target_id (TEXT), summary (JSONB — structured context, no secrets), request_id (TEXT — X-Request-ID from HTTP context), ip_address (TEXT — source IP, nullable for background tasks), prev_hash (TEXT — SHA-256 of previous event_hash for this actor or global chain), event_hash (TEXT NOT NULL — SHA-256(event_id + ts + actor_id + action + target_id + summary_canonical + prev_hash)). Partition by month (PostgreSQL declarative partitioning on ts). NO UPDATE or DELETE via ORM — enforced by database rule and ORM policy.

Step 2 - AuditLogger Service: Create services/discovery/src/discovery/services/audit_logger.py. Class AuditLogger. Method: log(session, actor: ActorContext, action: AuditAction, target_type: str, target_id: str, summary: dict, request_id: str = None). Computes: prev_hash by querying the latest event_hash where actor_id = actor.id (ordered by ts DESC). Computes event_hash. Inserts new row. All audit log calls are synchronous within the same DB transaction as the triggering action — audit and action commit together.

Step 3 - AuditAction Enum: Define a Python Enum class AuditAction with all permitted audit action strings. Include: CREATE_SERVICE, UPDATE_SERVICE, DEACTIVATE_SERVICE, CREATE_ENROLLMENT_TOKEN, APPROVE_ENROLLMENT_TOKEN, CANCEL_ENROLLMENT_TOKEN, SENTINEL_ONBOARDED, SENTINEL_DECOMMISSIONED, SENTINEL_REJOINED, ISSUE_CREDENTIAL, REVOKE_CREDENTIAL, ROTATE_CREDENTIAL, PUBLISH_STATUS_LIST, ANCHOR_STATUS_ON_CHAIN, ROLLBACK_CONFIG, ADMIN_LOGIN, ADMIN_LOGOUT, BREAK_GLASS_ACCESS, KEY_ROTATION. This list is exhaustive for MVP — new actions require code review.

Step 4 - Mandatory Integration: Every service / router that mutates state MUST call AuditLogger.log(). Enforced by: (a) base service class that requires audit_log parameter, (b) integration test that creates a resource without audit call and verifies test fails (negative test). Helper: @require_audit decorator for service methods.

Step 5 - Periodic Checkpoint: Background task (every 1 hour): compute a global chain checkpoint = SHA-256(last 1000 event_hashes joined). Store checkpoint in a separate audit_checkpoints table with timestamp and checkpoint_hash. Optionally anchor checkpoint_hash on-chain (TASK-031). This provides a coarse integrity anchor without requiring per-event on-chain writes.

Step 6 - Tamper Detection Endpoint: POST /api/v1/audit/verify-integrity (security-admin only). Steps: for a given time range, re-compute event_hash for each event by re-hashing content fields, compare against stored event_hash. Returns { events_checked, tampered_count, first_tampered_event_id }. This is a diagnostic tool — run on demand during investigations.

Step 7 - Export API: GET /api/v1/audit/events. Auth: security-admin. Query params: from (ISO-8601), to (ISO-8601), actor_id, action (multi-value), target_type, cursor (base64), limit (max 500). Returns paginated list of audit events with all fields (except raw secret fields). Response includes X-Total-Count header. Supports Accept: application/x-ndjson for streaming export.

Step 8 - Signed Export Bundle: POST /api/v1/audit/export. Auth: security-admin. Body: { from, to, include_hash_verification: bool }. Background job: collect all events for range → serialize to NDJSON → sign with Discovery issuer key as JWS → store in object storage or return as download URL. Status polling: GET /api/v1/audit/exports/{export_id}. Used for legal/forensic export.

Step 9 - Summary Fields Sanitization: summary JSONB field must NEVER contain: passwords, raw tokens, private keys, PII beyond actor_id, raw JWT content. Enforced by AuditLogger.sanitize_summary() which strips known sensitive keys and truncates values > 1000 chars. Test: unit test that passes a summary with a 'token' key and verifies it is redacted.

Step 10 - Retention Policy: Default: keep audit_events for 7 years (configurable per env: AUDIT_RETENTION_YEARS). Deletion only allowed via a separate privileged retention job with dual-admin approval (not via ORM). Partitioned tables make old partition archiving safe — archive to cold storage, then detach partition.

error_handling.behavior: The triggering action MUST also roll back (transactional). Log to stderr with CRITICAL level. Emit metric audit_write_failure_total. Do not silently continue without audit.
Return tampered events list. Emit metric audit_tamper_detected=1. Log to SIEM. Action: manual investigation required — system continues running.

security_constraints: Audit records are written atomically with the triggering action — no action can succeed without its audit record
Append-only: no UPDATE or DELETE ORM operations on audit_events (enforced by PostgreSQL RULE or audit_events trigger)
Hash chain allows detection of insertion, deletion, or modification of any audit record
summary field is sanitized to remove secrets — token values replaced with <REDACTED>
Export requires security-admin role and generates its own audit event: 'AUDIT_EXPORT_INITIATED'

acceptance_criteria: Every mutation endpoint (create service, approve token, revoke credential, etc.) produces an audit event in the same DB transaction
Two consecutive audit events have prev_hash of event N = event_hash of event N-1
POST /audit/verify-integrity on unmodified data returns tampered_count=0
Modifying an audit record directly in DB and running verify-integrity shows tampered_count > 0
Export API returns all events in date range with correct pagination
summary fields containing 'token', 'key', 'password' substrings are redacted in audit log
